---
title: "confusion_matrix"
generated: true
description: "Confusion matrix, `row = true` / `col = predicted` (sklearn form)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.metrics.classification`](../nitrix-metrics-classification.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:confusion_matrix -->
```python
confusion_matrix(pred: Int[Array, 'N'], target: Int[Array, 'N'], *, num_classes: int) -> Int[Array, 'C C']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:confusion_matrix -->
Confusion matrix, `row = true` / `col = predicted` (sklearn form).

`cm[i, j]` counts samples of true class `i` predicted as class `j`,
via `bincount(target * C + pred)`.  `num_classes` is static (it fixes
the `(C, C)` output shape, so the op is `jit`-clean).  Non-differentiable
(integer counts).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:confusion_matrix -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `pred` | `Int` | `N` | — | Integer predicted / true class indices `(N,)` in `[0, num_classes)`. |
| `target` | `Int` | `N` | — | Integer predicted / true class indices `(N,)` in `[0, num_classes)`. |
| `num_classes` | `int` | — | — | Number of classes `C` (static). |

**Returns** `Int` — shape `C` × `C`. The `(C, C)` integer count matrix, where entry `[i, j]` is the number of samples of true class `i` predicted as class `j`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:confusion_matrix -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `C` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
